cards.text: Malaysia hosts ~2.5 million documented migrant workers — and the US TIP Report keeps flagging the same forced-labour patterns.
cards.sub: ILO 2024 figure. Malaysia retained Tier 2 status in the 2025 TIP Report, with the State Department citing weak prosecution in palm oil and disposable glove manufacturing.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Employment Act 1955 amendments in 2022 extended core protections to foreign workers — enforcement remains the gap.
cards.s: Forced-labour indicators (passport confiscation, wage theft, debt bondage) appear across sectors. Enforcement targets individual workplaces, not systemic chains.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: The US TIP Report's Tier 2 status reflects increasing efforts but unmet minimum standards in several key areas.
cards.s: The 2025 report noted fewer trafficker convictions and no labour-trafficking prosecutions in palm oil or glove manufacturing despite ongoing reports.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Migrant workers anchor palm oil, electronics assembly, construction and plantations — the export base.
cards.s: Their total economic contribution is not aggregated in any single published figure. Main source countries: Bangladesh, Indonesia, Nepal, Myanmar.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Tier 2 is not a passing grade — it is a multi-year warning that the system documented in successive TIP reports has not been structurally fixed.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Like a factory that treats its workers' welfare as a compliance line item — essential to hire, not to protect.
cards.sub: Documentation buys access to labour but not enforcement of the protections already in law.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: MOUs and the 2022 Employment Act amendments are necessary but not sufficient. Tier 1 requires sectoral prosecutions in palm oil and gloves.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

issue_headline: Malaysia Has 2.5 Million Documented Migrant Workers — and Persistent Tier 2 Trafficking Concerns
